name: Pumpkin Ravioli (Hazen)
recipeIngredient: 1 medium pumpkin or large winter squash, halved and baked until soft (about 2-1/2 cups cooked)
2 large carrots, chopped and cooked until just soft
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pasta dough: If you can't find frozen pasta sheets or prefer to make your own, use this foolproof recipe. Knead herbs and spices compatible with the dish into the dough at the end.
- Make a well in the flour and add eggs to it. Beat together with a fork.
- Gradually stir the mixture into the flour from the bottom of the well until smooth or shiny.
- Incorporate the remaining flour gradually using your hands, kneading gently until the dough is smooth and resilient.
- Knead the dough until smooth and resilient; you may need to add more flour or it may be too soft to incorporate all the flour depending on humidity and egg size. If sticky or pliable, add more flour.
- Divide into three portions, cover with plastic wrap or an overturned bowl, and let rest at least 30 minutes.
2. Prepare the filling: Puree cooked pumpkin or squash and carrots in a food processor until smooth. Sauté onions, garlic, and spices in butter until onions are soft; add pureed vegetables.
- Add cheese, maple syrup, egg, salt, and pepper. Taste and adjust seasoning, then set aside.
3. Prepare the sauce: Toast hazelnuts in a shallow pan on the middle rack of an oven preheated to 400°F for 10-12 minutes or until brown and fragrant. Remove from heat; wrap tightly in a lint-free towel and rub vigorously.
- Open towel carefully and remove nuts, continuing to rub until nearly blond.
- Cook cream, garlic, cayenne, and white pepper over high heat, stirring often to avoid boiling over. When thick enough to coat spoon, add salt, taste, and adjust seasoning; set aside.
4. Assemble the ravioli: Roll dough very thin on a lightly floured surface or using a pasta machine (follow manufacturer's instructions for sheets about 1 millimeter thick).
- Lay one sheet on a flat surface, spray with water to prevent drying, and place filling in rows approximately 1/2 inch apart.
- Fold over the filling and cut into ravioli shapes. Place finished ravioli in a damp cloth; cook in salted boiling water until al dente.
5. Finish serving: Reheat sauce, add shredded sorrel (about 30 seconds), half the hazelnuts, turn off heat, and gently incorporate cooked ravioli. Serve immediately garnished with remaining nuts.
